curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: sine, cosine and tangent ratios in right-angled triangles (invariance under scaling)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Understand and explain sin, cos and tan as side-length ratios in right-angled triangles, and show that these ratios stay constant when similar triangles are scaled.

verse_ality.driving_question: Why do sine, cosine and tangent stay the same even when a right-angled triangle gets bigger or smaller?

knowing_world.focus: Explore sine, cosine and tangent as ratios of side lengths in right-angled triangles and investigate how these ratios remain constant when the triangle is scaled.
key_concepts: Opposite, adjacent and hypotenuse (relative to an angle)
Invariant ratios for a fixed angle

human_story.focus: Connect trigonometry to navigation and exploration: using triangles to measure what can’t be reached directly, from sea voyages to mapping and astronomy.
justice_prompts: Imagine an ancient navigator using just triangles for voyages. What could she measure and why would it matter?

expressive_self.focus: Create a personal interpretation of a trig ratio and communicate the idea of a constant relationship as a triangle scales.
activities: Create your own interpretation of a trigonometric ratio (doodle, metaphor, storyboard, or poem).
If cosine could speak, what story would it tell about its relationship to angle and structure?

designed_world.focus: Apply triangle ratios to design and engineering contexts (ramps, camera angles, sound modelling) and think about how to make maths tools accessible for different learners.
design_challenges: Buddy challenge: Design a ramp rising 0.5 m over 6 m horizontally. What angle does that ratio imply?
Ethical design question: How might you adapt your sketches or explanations to support someone with low vision or color-blindness?

regulation_supports: Breathe deeply: as you breathe in, imagine drawing a triangle’s first side; hold, draw the next; breathe out, draw the third.
wellbeing_prompts: Can you feel the ratio staying balanced even as the shape expands?

pathways.name: Final Task: Trigonometry Presentation
pathways.focus: Create and share a presentation, diagram, or creative reflection that explains one trig ratio, demonstrates invariance under scaling, and links it to a real-world or creative context.
products: Explanation of one ratio (sine, cosine, or tangent) with correct notation.
Evidence (screenshots/diagrams) showing the ratio stays the same as the triangle scales.
A link to a real-world design context or a creative interpretation.
criteria: Conceptual Understanding: depth of grasp on ratio ideas.
Fluency & Accuracy: precision in notation and calculations.
Application to Contexts: effectiveness using maths in real-world design.

required: Identify the hypotenuse in a right-angled triangle
Comfort labelling opposite and adjacent relative to a chosen angle

builds_toward: Trig ratios to find missing sides
Inverse trig to find missing angles
Trig ratios for larger angles (unit circle/quadrants)
